af_score_components.notes: MCP server via mcp-atlassian (quality 72) covers both Jira and Confluence. Two REST API versions (v1 and v2) with different payloads — v2 is preferred but documentation is split. Content stored as Confluence storage format (XHTML variant), not Markdown. Rate limits poorly documented with undocumented endpoint-specific limits. OAuth2 adds complexity for agent auth — API tokens are simpler for server-side agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced, FedRAMP authorized. OAuth2 with product-level scopes (read/write per product). API tokens for simple server auth. Atlassian account tokens shared across Jira/Confluence. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR/FedRAMP — comprehensive compliance. Webhook delivery not guaranteed exactly-once — handlers must be idempotent.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Atlassian Cloud SLA (99.9%+) with comprehensive status page. Active v1→v2 migration creates documentation fragmentation. No retry guidance. No idempotency on page creation — duplicate pages created on retry. Rate limit 429s on bulk operations require exponential backoff. Space key and page ID lookups required before most write operations.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Excellent documentation. ACL token via X-Consul-Token header is simple for agents. ACLs may be disabled in dev/test deployments — production must enable. Blocking queries (long-poll via index-based polling) are powerful but require understanding. Multi-datacenter queries need explicit DC routing. Full idempotency on KV operations.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS configurable with mutual TLS support (deployment-dependent). Fine-grained ACL policies per service, node, key prefix, and intent. ACLs not enabled by default in development mode — production deployments must explicitly enable. HashiCorp maintained with SOC2/FedRAMP for enterprise tier. Vault integration available for token management.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: HCP Consul (managed) has SLA; self-hosted is operator's responsibility. Very stable API with strong backward compatibility across major versions. Blocking queries provide efficient long-polling without hammering the API. Full idempotency on KV writes. Retry guidance documented. Raft consensus provides strong consistency guarantees.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server available (quality 72). Excellent docs with REST + GraphQL. Two separate API keys (CDA read-only, CMA write) is good security design but requires management. Publishing requires explicit publish call after create/update — easy to create unpublished content accidentally. Entry versioning causes optimistic locking conflicts on concurrent edits. 10 req/sec CMA rate limit documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Strong security design: separate read-only (CDA) and write (CMA) tokens. CMA tokens scoped per space. OAuth2 for multi-user apps. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR. CDN-delivered content requires cache invalidation consideration. Webhook payload signatures should be validated.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: CDN + CMS SLA with status page. Stable versioning. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Entry version conflicts require read-modify-write retry pattern. Webhook delivery not exactly-once. GraphQL schema changes automatically when content model changes — plan accordingly.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with moderate quality (65). Requires running multiple services: Qdrant, Redis, embedding server, LLM decoder, file watcher. Ports 8000-8003, 8080, 8100-8101, 6333-6334, 6379 all must be available. BUSL-1.1 license restricts commercial use. Moderate search accuracy (MRR 0.276 on CoSQA). VS Code extension temporarily unavailable. Zero-auth local mode reduces friction.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local deployment has no TLS by default. Optional session authentication with zero-auth mode for local use. No scope granularity. BUSL-1.1 license creates usage uncertainty. Unknown dependency audit status. Multiple exposed ports increase local attack surface. Not designed for production security hardening.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted, no SLA. Unknown version — unstable API surface. Multiple service dependencies create high failure probability. No documented breaking change policy. Moderate search accuracy is a reliability concern for code intelligence use cases. Suitable for development environments only.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server for multi-service integration (quality 60). Centralized OAuth management via HashiCorp Vault is the key value proposition. Early stage project (104 commits) with rough edges. AGPL v3 license restricts commercial derivative works. Phase 2/3 roadmap features (context memory, semantic retrieval) not yet available. Limited to ~14 supported services.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced on hosted endpoint. JWT + OAuth2 with per-service scopes. HashiCorp Vault for credential storage is a strong architectural choice. Early stage with unknown dependency audit status. AGPL may create compliance concerns for enterprise use. Good credential isolation design despite early stage.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Early stage project with no SLA. Unknown version — high probability of breaking changes. Roadmap features missing. Unknown idempotency behavior. Single integration platform creates a single point of failure for all connected services. Not ready for production use.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with good quality (80). Free tier requires no auth — very low friction for getting live documentation. Requires explicit 'use context7' invocation unless client rules configured. Community-contributed docs have no accuracy guarantee. Library resolution may return multiple matches requiring disambiguation. OAuth only for remote HTTP (not stdio). Upstash-backed service.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced on hosted service. Optional API key auth with no scope granularity. No formal compliance certifications. Upstash-backed with reasonable operational security. Community-contributed documentation means content quality is not security-audited. Appropriate for non-sensitive code assistance use cases.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Hosted by Upstash with reasonable but undocumented SLA. Unknown version. Community contributions mean library coverage and freshness vary. Proprietary backend parsing engine creates dependency on their service for doc freshness. Full GET idempotency. No retry guidance. Suitable for development assistance, not mission-critical pipelines.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with decent quality (70). Entirely local via stdio — no network exposure. 11 MCP tools covering structural analysis, semantic search, blast radius, and safe modification. Requires local Ollama with embedding model. Creates .mcp_data/ cache directory. Shadow restore points are separate from git history — two undo systems to manage. propose_commit is the only code write path.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local stdio MCP server — no network exposure (positive for security). Ollama API key via env var. No scope granularity within the tool. Unknown version and dependency audit status. Shadow restore points provide safe rollback but are separate from version control. Open source with no compliance certifications.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted, no SLA. Unknown version — API surface may change. No documented deprecation policy. Shadow restore points provide recovery from bad modifications. Requires local Ollama instance for embeddings — adds operational dependency. Partial idempotency. Good fit for individual developer use, not team production workflows.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Two API versions (v3 with API key, v4 with OAuth2 scopes) — select one and don't mix. Creator-focused platform with clean subscriber/tag model. Rate limit (120 req/min) is adequate for most use cases. Webhook signature verification with shared secret. v4 OAuth2 recommended for new integrations.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. v4 OAuth2 with scopes is the recommended auth path. v3 API key is account-scoped with no granularity — avoid for new integrations. GDPR/CAN-SPAM compliance. Webhook signature verification available — must validate to prevent spoofing. v4 scopes limit blast radius.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with status page and reasonable SLA. Both v3 and v4 currently supported. v3→v4 migration documented. Cursor-based pagination. No retry guidance documented. Webhook delivery not exactly-once — handlers should be idempotent. Rate limit headroom adequate for most creator automation workloads.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server/manager (quality 55). VS Code-only extension — no value outside the editor. Requires GitHub Copilot Chat subscription — double dependency. Error message quality is 0 per existing evaluation — critical gap. GPL-3.0 license restricts commercial use. Vendored code from anomalyco/opencode with unclear provenance. Skills registry (skills.sh) is the main value-add.

security_score_components.security_notes: GitHub Copilot OAuth provides established authentication. Vendored code with unclear provenance is a supply chain concern. GPL-3.0 license has copyleft implications. No scope granularity within extension. Cloud MCP remote option uses OAuth. VS Code extension sandbox limits some attack surface.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: VS Code extension with no independent SLA. v0.0.91 — early stage with expected instability. Depends on both VS Code and GitHub Copilot Chat being available. No documented breaking change policy. Extension and Copilot subscription requirements create multiple failure points. Suitable only for VS Code + Copilot users.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Documentation behind Coupa Compass customer portal — requires login to access. Each tenant has unique subdomain URL requiring per-customer config. API version must be specified in requests. Approval workflows are business-process driven — API actions may trigger multi-step human approvals. Sandbox credentials separate from production. Enterprise-only pricing.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 + API keys with module-level scopes (PO, invoices, suppliers). SOC1/SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR — strong enterprise compliance suite. Tenant-specific URLs and credentials prevent cross-tenant data access. Sandbox/production credential separation is required practice. Enterprise-grade security posture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Enterprise SaaS with contractual SLA. API versioning required but multi-version support exists. Sandbox/production separation prevents accidents. Approval workflows create async state machines that agents must navigate. Partial idempotency. No retry guidance documented. PO creation requires deep knowledge of Coupa data model.
